'''Anarak IV''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Anarak system/Legends|Anarak system]] of the [[Wyl sector/Legends|Wyl sector]].

==History==
Discovered during the [[Mandalorian Wars]], the planet was taken by the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] and eventually fell to [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Empire]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. Contested over during the [[New Sith Wars]], the planet remained independent following the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]],<ref name="TEA" /> when the ruling [[Wikipedia:Tetrarch|tetrarch]] claimed total control over the system.<ref name="MSII" />

Remaining neutral during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], an individual from Anarak IV was serving the Republic in the capacity of a chef on [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]]. At the [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit/Legends|Republic Mobile Surgical Unit's]] mess hall, this individual served several different species several different dishes to please the varying palates. [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] members [[Kaird]], [[Thula]], and [[Squa Tront]] commented that if this chef cooked for the tetrarch of his homeworld, he would likely be boiled alive.<ref name="MSII" />

A member state of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the world avoided the atrocities of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. As of [[137 ABY]], Anarak IV was a member of [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Appearances==
*''[[MedStar II: Jedi Healer]]'' {{Mo}}
